tavily.answer: In Tanzania, the United Nations’ food agencies have warned that acute hunger is set to worsen across 13 global hotspots, including Tanzania, due to conflict, funding shortages, and climate shocks. The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) and the World Food Program (WFP) highlighted that conditions are expected to deteriorate between June and November 2026, with around 266 million people facing high levels of acute food insecurity. In northern Tanzania, Maasai women are turning drought-resistant grass into animal feed and income, transforming a survival tactic into a climate-adaptation business coordinated by the Pastoral Women’s Council (PWC).
